description_long: This object is a cluster of large, trumpet-shaped flowers with delicate, pale pink petals that have darker pink and red streaks radiating from the center. The petals are broad, slightly overlapping, and have gently ruffled edges, giving the blooms a lush and full appearance. The central part of each flower features prominent greenish-yellow stamens and a subtle gradient of color toward the throat. The flowers are attached to a thick, green stem, which is smooth and cylindrical, and a few elongated, lanceolate leaves are visible near the base. The back view reveals the greenish undersides of the petals and the structure of the flower cluster. This type of flower is often seen in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative landscaping along sidewalks.
description: A cluster of large, pale pink trumpet-shaped flowers with ruffled petals and a thick green stem, featuring prominent stamens and subtle color gradients. The blooms are lush and slightly overlapping, with a few elongated leaves at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open faces of the pink flowers, with visible stamens and overlapping petals.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the greenish undersides of the petals and the structure of the flower cluster.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the layered arrangement of the petals and the thickness of the stem.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the full cluster of flowers and the radial arrangement of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, with each flower displaying overlapping and slightly ruffled petals that radiate symmetrically from the center. The arrangement creates a full, layered appearance typical of many ornamental flowers.
